data.goal: Mine exactly 8 stone, 10 coal ore, 3 iron ore; get crafting table; craft/place furnace; smelt 3 iron; craft iron pickaxe
data.reasoning: Failed dig for stone at (106,145,-36): expected drop not acquired. Nearby_blocks shows stone at (108,145,-36) not (106,...), so target was stale/wrong/air. Agent at y=142 looking straight up; an item entity is ~8 blocks away at y=136 (drop likely fell). Inventory only has 1 cobblestone. Category: action/environment (bad dig target + missed pickup). Not a resource or fatal planning flaw—ores are still visible nearby. Retry with corrected targeting and explicit pickup; do not give up.

subtasks.description: Path to and collect nearby item entity at ~ (108,136,-36) and any other drops
Re-scan for stone; dig valid stone until 8 cobblestone (need 8 total; have 1)
Mine 10 coal_ore with stone pickaxe (visible near 103,145,-32)
Craft planks from dark_oak_log if needed, make/place crafting table
Craft/place furnace, smelt 3 raw iron with coal, craft iron pickaxe

actions.analysis: Dig targeted stone at (106,145,-36) but that block was missing/stale (nearby stone is at 108,145,-36). Drop was not acquired; item entity exists below at y=136—either wrong block, already broken, or drops fell and were not picked up. Pitch straight up and stand position also suboptimal for that target.
actions.suggestion: Do not re-dig same coords. Re-query nearby blocks, path close to valid stone/ore, look at block, dig, then immediately move to collect drops. Keep stone pickaxe selected.
actions.new_approach: 1) Collect nearby item drops first. 2) Face and dig fresh scanned stone positions until 8 cobble. 3) Then coal_ore x10 and iron_ore x3 from current nearby positions. 4) Use log for planks/table if no table; furnace; smelt; craft iron pick.
